metadata.description: Threat actor attribution dataset for edge security vendors. Covers named APT groups, campaigns, and nation-state attributions linked to exploitation of edge/perimeter devices (firewalls, VPN gateways, ADCs) from 11 vendors.
metadata.key_statistic: Mandiant M-Trends 2025: exploits remain the dominant initial access vector at 33% of all investigated incidents; all four most frequently exploited vulnerabilities in 2024 were in edge devices. Google Threat Intelligence Group: 44% of zero-days exploited in 2024 targeted security and edge appliances.

actors.name: UNC3886
actors.description: China-nexus espionage group specializing in edge device and hypervisor exploitation. Deploys custom TINYSHELL-based backdoors with deep knowledge of FortiOS, Junos OS, and VMware ESXi internals. Deployed 6 custom TINYSHELL variants on Juniper MX Series carrier-grade routers (disclosed March 2025). Targets include ISPs, telecoms, and US defense contractors. Demonstrates vendor-agnostic specialization returning across different vendor products over multiple years.

actors.name: UNC5221
actors.description: China-nexus espionage actor with persistent focus on Ivanti products. Responsible for the January 2024 Ivanti crisis (~1,700 appliances compromised), triggering CISA Emergency Directive ED 24-01. Returned with new exploits throughout 2024-2025. Linked to the F5 BIG-IP corporate breach (Aug 2025) via BRICKSTORM malware, where source code and undisclosed vulnerability information were exfiltrated after 12+ months of persistent access. Malware families include the SPAWN family (SPAWNANT, SPAWNSNARE, SPAWNSLOTH), DRYHOOK, PHASEJAM, TRAILBLAZE, BRUSHFIRE, and BRICKSTORM.

actors.name: UNC5337
actors.description: China-nexus threat cluster attributed by Mandiant to exploitation of Ivanti Connect Secure CVE-2025-0282. Whether UNC5337 represents a distinct actor from UNC5221 or the same cluster at a different operational phase is not resolved in public reporting. Both groups focus exclusively on Ivanti edge devices.

actors.name: UTA0218
actors.description: State-nexus threat actor responsible for Operation MidnightEclipse. Exploited Palo Alto PAN-OS GlobalProtect zero-day CVE-2024-3400 (CVSS 10.0), the #1 most frequently exploited vulnerability in Mandiant M-Trends 2025. Deployed UPSTYLE backdoor. Volexity discovered active exploitation before any patch existed.

actors.name: UAT4356
actors.description: State-sponsored espionage group with deep specialization in Cisco ASA/FTD products. Burned 4 zero-days across two campaigns (2024, 2025). ArcaneDoor campaign (Nov 2023 - Apr 2024) targeted government networks globally using Line Dancer (memory-resident shellcode interpreter) and Line Runner (persistent backdoor surviving reboots). 2025 campaign introduced RayInitiator bootkit and LINE VIPER shellcode loader. Censys research found links to Chinese networks and anti-censorship software on actor infrastructure.

actors.name: Volt Typhoon
actors.description: PRC state-sponsored group focused on pre-positioning in US critical infrastructure for potential disruption during a geopolitical conflict. Uses living-off-the-land techniques post-compromise. Has the broadest vendor coverage of any single China-nexus actor in this dataset — documented exploitation across Fortinet, Ivanti, Citrix, Cisco, and Sophos. Identified in the Sophos Pacific Rim report as an overlapping TTP cluster. Targets exclusively critical infrastructure: energy, water, telecommunications, and transportation.

actors.name: Earth Estries
actors.description: Conducts the largest documented edge device campaign: 600+ organizations breached across 80+ countries since 2019. Heavy focus on telecommunications providers including multiple US carriers (October 2024 disclosure). Specific CVE-level attribution has not been publicly enumerated but exploitation of multiple vendors' edge devices is confirmed.

actors.name: APT41
actors.description: Dual-purpose Chinese threat group conducting both state-sponsored espionage and financially motivated operations. Exploited Citrix CVE-2019-19781 in a wide-ranging global intrusion campaign (Jan-Mar 2020). Identified as an overlapping TTP cluster in the Sophos Pacific Rim report. Also targeted Cisco devices in the same 2020 campaign.

actors.name: APT31
actors.description: China-linked espionage group that exploited Sophos CyberoamOS vulnerability CVE-2020-29574 and was identified as part of the Pacific Rim threat cluster targeting Sophos edge devices over a five-year period.

actors.name: DriftingCloud
actors.description: Chinese APT group that exploited Sophos Firewall zero-day CVE-2022-1040 weeks before patch availability. Used compromised firewalls for man-in-the-middle attacks by modifying DNS responses. Targeting concentrated in South Asia (Afghanistan, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka). Malware deployed includes PupyRAT, Pantegana, and Sliver.

actors.name: TA413
actors.description: China-nexus group targeting Tibetan government-in-exile organizations. Deployed custom LOWZERO backdoor through compromised Sophos firewalls exploited via CVE-2022-1040.

actors.name: UNC5174
actors.description: Former hacktivist collective member assessed as an MSS contractor. Functions as an initial access broker selling access to compromised F5 BIG-IP appliances to US defense contractors and UK government. Exploited F5 BIG-IP CVE-2023-46747 (Oct-Nov 2023).

actors.name: UNC5027
actors.description: Threat cluster tracked by Mandiant responsible for backdooring at least 2,000 Citrix NetScaler devices before a patch existed for CVE-2023-3519. Planted webshells and PHP backdoors for persistent access. CISA advisory AA23-201A documented the pre-patch mass exploitation campaign in July 2023.

actors.name: BOLDMOVE Actor
actors.description: Suspected China-nexus actor that developed custom BOLDMOVE malware purpose-built for FortiGate firewall internals, with both Windows and Linux/FortiOS variants. Exploited CVE-2022-42475 as a zero-day targeting a European government and an African managed service provider.

actors.name: Pioneer Kitten
actors.description: Iran-based cyber actor associated with the Government of Iran. Exploited Check Point VPN zero-day CVE-2024-24919 (May-Aug 2024) and Fortinet VPN credentials. Distinctive operational model: state-sponsored actors who monetize access by collaborating with ransomware affiliates (AlphV/BlackCat, RansomHouse, NoEscape). Within 48 hours of PoC publication for CVE-2024-24919, over 10,000 devices were actively scanned. Blurs the line between nation-state espionage and criminal ransomware.
sources: Department of Defense Cyber Crime Center (DC3)

actors.name: Sandworm-linked actors
actors.description: Tooling associated with Russia's GRU Unit 74455 linked to exploitation of Zyxel firewall CVE-2022-30525 for persistent access to edge devices, likely in the context of Ukraine-related operations. The exact operational unit may be a subcluster or affiliate rather than the core Sandworm team.

actors.name: LockBit
actors.description: Prolific ransomware-as-a-service operation. Affiliates exploited CitrixBleed CVE-2023-4966 in high-profile attacks on Boeing, ICBC (disrupting US Treasury markets), DP World, and Allen & Overy. Also targeted Fortinet VPN infrastructure. The ICBC breach produced the highest-impact single edge device incident in this dataset.

actors.name: Akira
actors.description: Ransomware group that systematically exploited SonicWall SSL VPN CVE-2024-40766 for initial access. Responsible for approximately 75% of documented SonicWall ransomware intrusions (30+ per Arctic Wolf). Full network encryption achieved in under 10 hours. Active campaign from September 2024 through at least August 2025, with renewed uptick in July 2025 against the same year-old CVE.

actors.name: Fog
actors.description: Ransomware group that exploited SonicWall SSL VPN CVE-2024-40766 alongside Akira, responsible for approximately 25% of documented SonicWall intrusions. Primarily targets education and SMB sectors in the United States.

actors.name: Helldown
actors.description: Double-extortion ransomware group that weaponized Zyxel path traversal CVE-2024-11667 to steal VPN credentials and establish backdoor connections before lateral movement and encryption. 31 victims listed on extortion portal.

actors.name: Cring
actors.description: Ransomware group that specifically targeted unpatched Fortinet FortiGate VPN servers via CVE-2018-13379 to gain initial access to industrial and manufacturing networks in Europe in 2021.

actors.name: Mirai botnet operators
actors.description: Multiple Mirai-variant botnet operators mass-exploited Zyxel firewall vulnerabilities in 2023 for DDoS botnet recruitment. Approximately 24,500 exposed devices per Censys at time of exploitation. Represents the commodity/botnet threat tier: mass scanning for newly-patched CVEs, exploiting before the long tail of unpatched devices closes.
